title: Scorching 44°C: NSG Officer Collapses - Infomance
full_text:
A viral video has surfaced showing a National Security Guard (NSG) commando collapsing due to a heatstroke during a public event in Andhra Pradesh. The commando, part of Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu’s security detail, fainted while the CM was addressing a gathering near an Anna Canteen. Officials standing nearby quickly intervened to catch the commando before he hit the ground. The incident highlights the severe environmental conditions currently affecting the region, where temperatures have soared to a staggering 44°C.
According to the Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA), a heatwave is currently prevailing across 32 mandals. While light rainfall is predicted for districts like Srikakulam and Vizianagaram between April 20 and 23, dry and grueling weather is expected to persist in Visakhapatnam and East Godavari. In neighboring Telangana, the situation is equally critical, with the Telangana Development Planning Society reporting that 10 districts have already exceeded the 44°C mark, while 13 others remain between 43°C and 44°C.
Adding to the climatic volatility, a severe storm alert has been issued for several Telangana districts, including Medchal and Yadadri, following the prolonged heatwave. Authorities have urged citizens to take necessary precautions as the combination of extreme heat and impending storms creates hazardous conditions. The fainting of a highly trained elite commando serves as a stark reminder of the physical toll the current weather patterns are taking on those working outdoors.
